name: Economy (Soft)
description: Daily-driver fuel-economy tune. Target: -5%..-8% fuel consumption. Stock peak torque preserved (~400 Nm), peak power slightly reduced (~5%). Strategy: lift low-mid RPM torque (driver shifts up earlier), trim upper RPM torque (driver avoids high-rev band), raise rail pressure (better atomisation, more efficient burn). EGR stays ON (critical for fuel economy). No hardware modification required, hardware-safe.

edits.comment: +8% low-mid RPM (1250-1700 rpm). Driver upshifts earlier, engine cruises at lower RPM = fuel saved. Cap 7500 = stock peak (400 Nm limit preserved).
-7% upper RPM (3500-4100 rpm). When driver pushes the pedal, torque feels weak so they instinctively avoid the high-rev band. Caps the fuel peak.
+8% rail pressure in mid-high load region. Smaller fuel droplets = more complete combustion = more work from the same fuel. Also reduces smoke. Cap 1380 bar (CP1 pump safe).
+3% boost in low-mid RPM band. Light torque assist = driver presses pedal less = less fuel. Cap 2300 mbar (turbo-safe).
-8% transient fuel in high-load region. When driver mashes the pedal the engine feels 'unresponsive', they naturally back off = fuel saved. Subtle 'eco-mode' effect.
-8% main fuel at 3000+ rpm high-load region. Upper-RPM performance is constrained = driver doesn't 'work the engine', cruise stays in 1500-2500 rpm band.

options.label: Keep EGR active (REQUIRED for Economy mode)
options.description: This option must remain OFF (i.e. EGR stays ON) for Economy mode to work. EGR is a critical part of fuel economy: it recycles exhaust gas, improves heat distribution, lowers combustion temperature, and net-reduces fuel consumption. Disabling EGR breaks Economy mode - in this variant EGR-off is COUNTER-PRODUCTIVE.

options.label: Soft rev-limit (gentle taper at 3800 rpm)
options.description: Optional: cuts the torque-limiter cells at 3700-4100 rpm by an additional 15%. The engine 'softly brakes' as the driver leans on the pedal near 3800 rpm, prompting an upshift naturally. Useful for more aggressive economy. Works well on manual transmissions; not needed on automatics.

edits.comment: Additional -15% at upper RPM, creates a soft rev-limit feel.
